description: Shape of the JSON emitted by 03_scripts/runtime_activation/check_ghoti_runtime.ps1. The check is local and read-only; it starts no agent, opens no network connection, and reads no secret values (only whether secret files are present).

fields.ok: bool - overall: a working Python was resolved and the status brain + status bridge scripts are present.
fields.repo_root: string - resolved repository root for this worktree.
fields.origin_main_short: string|null - short hash of origin/main (read-only git lookup), or null if unavailable.
fields.python_ok: bool - a working Python interpreter was resolved (broken PATH shim is skipped).
fields.telegram_bot_scripts_present: bool - the status-only Telegram bot scripts are present.
fields.telegram_secret_files_present: bool - both the token file and allowed-chat-id file exist OUTSIDE the repo (their contents are never read).
fields.hermes_wsl_available: bool - the wsl launcher is available on this host.
fields.ollama_available: bool - the local ollama CLI is available on this host.
fields.gemma_available: bool - a local gemma model is listed by ollama (best-effort, may be false if ollama is not running).
fields.same_session_id: string - the WSL Hermes session id this runtime resumes (20260601_081506_d35c70).
fields.runtime_config_path: string - the LOCAL (outside-repo) Telegram runtime config path the enable script writes.
